title: pycurl C Extension Analysis Report
author: devdanzin
body:
I've run a [C extension code review](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit) tool on pycurl and got an interesting report. The main findings were:

> The analysis uncovered a **use-after-free in `do_share_new`** (double-free of lock after `Py_DECREF` frees the object), a **deadlock on Python 3.13+** from a leaked lock in `share_cleanup_and_count_live_easies`, a **BytesIO leak on every failed `perform_rb()` call**, **`PyLong_AsLong` overflow unchecked in 3 setopt functions** (returns success with pending exception), a **missing `Py_INCREF(Py_None)` in `do_multi_setopt_charpp`** (refcount corruption on 3.10-3.11), **`CurlMulti.tp_traverse` missing callback members** (GC cycle leak), and **`PyObject_New` guarded only by `assert`** in 2 locations.

The full report is at https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/7402dc7349ab7e27deb95093c1ddb883. I hope it helps.

comments.author: swt2c
comments.body: Thanks a lot, I believe I've fixed most of the issues it reported.  If you have a chance to run it again, let me know.  Closing this for now.
